title: fine dining lunch kl
full_text: Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Now Reading: 6 Fine Dining Restaurants With Luxe Lunch Menus You Can’t Say No To 1 01 6 Fine Dining Restaurants With Luxe Lunch Menus You Can’t Say No To Main// Eat// Drink// Reviews// Insider// At Home// About Us// Download Our Magazine// Q2 2026// Q1 2026// Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026// Home Eat 6 Fine Dining Restaurants With Luxe Lunch Menus You Can’t Say No To6 Fine Dining Restaurants With Luxe Lunch Menus You Can’t Say No ToPatrick LohEat1 month agoShare From wagyu to lobster, these six fine dining spots deliver satisfying midday resets that feel indulgent without slowing you down. There are days when you’re wedged between a packed workday, trying to stay locked in, and a deadline that feels personally offended by your existence. Your energy is low, your colleague is one email away from collapse, and only a very good lunch stands between you and a full-blown headache. On the rare, blessed occasions when the office is a little quieter, consider stretching that lunch just enough to feel human again. So where do you go for something impressive enough to reset your mood, indulgent enough to feel like a treat, but not so heavy it drains your monthly fine dining allowance? We’ve got you. Here are six fine-dining restaurants to escape to for a satisfying, indulgent lunch before you slip back to that spreadsheet like nothing happened. Nobu KL The Hanami Bento Box includes Whitefish and Tuna.Photo: Courtesy of Nobu KL. Nobu is known for lunch sets that manage to be generous without slowing you down. Think sizable portions that arrive quickly, disappear even quicker, and leave you surprisingly functional for your 2pm meeting. From the à la carte lunch menu, there’s Japanese Wagyu, plus the Pre Fixe Lunch set starting from RM190. If speed is the priority, go straight for the Matsuhisa Bento Box, Kuala Lumpur Lunch Bento Box, Hanami Bento Box, or the Set Lunch & Donburi plates. They’re built for efficiency without feeling rushed. Don’t skip the fresh sashimi sets either, as they do a lot of heavy lifting here. IG: @nobu_kualalumpur DC Restaurant Quail, sourced directly from Foshan in Guangdong, China.Photo: Courtesy of DC Restaurant. If your lunch strategy involves impressing a client, then DC Restaurant is your move. The menu changes every season, shifting with French techniques and multicultural influences, so nothing feels on autopilot. You can choose between a three-course or four-course lunch, starting from RM358. On the menu: Ofunato Bay Scallops, Slow Roasted Rack of Lamb, Foshan Quail, A5 Miyazaki Chateaubriand, and Brittany Bleu Lobster Tail with Nduja Gratin. Lunch ends with Le Fromage and at that point, the deal is probably already done. IG: @dc_restaurant Skillet KL Pan-seared dry-aged Cherry Valley duck, served with preserved calamansi jus.Photo: Courtesy of Skillet KL. Right in the middle of KL, where everyone is half-running, half-texting, Skillet KL feels like a controlled pause button. Here, lunch comes in options depending on your hunger level and how much time you have. The two-course includes an appetiser and a main; the three-course adds dessert; and the four-course gives you everything plus an extra appetiser. Prices start from RM138. Beverage pairing is available from RM88 if you’re leaning into the experience. If you’re going green, their vegetarian menu holds its own. Standouts like Celeriac Dauphinoise and Salt Baked Jicama are the kind of servings that quietly convert people. IG: @skillet_kl Potager KL Seared pigeon with butter.Photo: Courtesy of Potager KL. Potager KL is built on a farm-to-table philosophy, but crafted with a spectacular French vision. The signature lunch menu is a five-course experience at RM490, featuring dishes like lobster, pigeon, seabream, lamb, and wagyu (with an additional RM120). If the afternoon calls for something extra, there’s pairing to match. The Camellia Path offers five glasses at RM400, while the non-alcohol option, Orchid Passage, gives you five glasses at RM200. Either way, the lunch is there to upgrade your afternoon. IG: @potager.kl Entier French Dining Roasted lamb rack.Photo: Courtesy of Entier French Dining. Entier is what happens when you want to mentally clock out of your desk but still stay in Kuala Lumpur. No passport required. Lunch here comes in two options: a two-course set (RM148) and a three-course set (RM168). The two-course lets you choose either a starter or dessert with a main, while the three-course gives you the full line-up. For mains, the recommendations lean confidently toward Poulet Grand-Mère, Roasted Lamb Rack, Lobster & Pasta, and Bouillabaisse. Drinks are included, so you can choose from house-made sodas or teas like Japanese Sencha, Lapsang Souchong, Cinnamon Spice, and Vanilla Grapefruit. It’s the kind of lunch that makes going back to your inbox slightly less painful. IG: @entierfrenchdining Nadodi Lightly cured scallops, smoked and grilled over charcoal.Photo: Courtesy of Nadodi. When the midday craving calls for South Asian fine dining with a little swagger, Nadodi answers loud and clear. Drawing inspiration from the wandering food cultures of Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and Sri Lanka, the restaurant turns age-old coastal flavours into plates that feel both rooted and wildly refined. Expect dishes like Line-Caught Snapper with Dashi Rasam, Mount Schanck Lamb, Smoked & Cured Scallops, and Chettinad Crab Tarts from the lunch set menu (RM298), all designed to keep conversation briefly suspended. And if lunch needs a liquid co-star, their alcoholic pairings start from RM120. Midday meetings can wait.
